title: ERP CORE
description: ERP CORE is a comprehensive open-access resource for event-related potential (ERP) research, providing raw EEG data from 40 neurotypical participants across 6 task paradigms designed to elicit 7 widely-used ERP components (N170, MMN, N2pc, N400, P3, LRP, and ERN). The dataset includes optimized experimental paradigms, stimulus materials, demographic information, and standardized data processing pipelines to facilitate reproducible ERP research and component characterization.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 40 neurotypical adults across 6 task paradigms administered in a single recording session: an Eriksen flanker task (ERN/LRP), passive auditory oddball (MMN), face perception task (N170), visual search task (N2pc), word pair judgment task (N400), and active visual oddball (P3). EEG coordinate system uses EEGLAB orientation (Anterior-Left-Superior).
